meaning_en: To leave someone or something behind completely
example: She had to abandon her car in the snow and walk home.
mnemonic: A BAND ON a sinking ship — the band plays as everyone abandons it.

meaning_en: At a higher position or level than something else
example: The plane flew above the clouds.
mnemonic: AB + OVE — ABove is where OVEr things go.

meaning_en: From one side to the other side of something
example: She walked across the bridge to reach the other side.
mnemonic: A CROSS lies ACROSS the road — you must cross it.

meaning_en: Something done to achieve a result or purpose
example: We need to take action before the problem gets worse.
mnemonic: ACTion — when you ACT, you do an action.

meaning_en: Something you do as a hobby, job, or for a purpose
example: Swimming is her favourite outdoor activity.
mnemonic: ACTIVITY = ACTIVE + ITY — being active is an activity.

meaning_en: To put something together with something else to increase the amount
example: Add some sugar to the tea to make it sweeter.
mnemonic: ADD two numbers — always makes more.

meaning_en: The details of where someone lives or works
example: Please write your home address on the form.
mnemonic: ADD + RESS — you ADD your location so letters can REACH you.

meaning_en: A fully grown person, usually 18 years old or older
example: Only adults are allowed inside the club.
mnemonic: ADULT — someone who has ADULThood; fully grown.

meaning_en: Feeling fear or worry about something
example: She was afraid of the dark when she was little.
mnemonic: A FRAID — a FRAUD scares you; you become afraid.

meaning_en: Extremely surprising and impressive
example: The view from the top of the mountain was amazing.
mnemonic: AMAZING — makes you say 'Ahhh!' in a maze of wonder.

meaning_en: Feeling strong displeasure or annoyance
example: He was angry when he found out his phone was broken.
mnemonic: ANGRY — an ANGel's opposite; when you're not calm, you're angry.

meaning_en: A living creature that is not a plant or human
example: A dog is a friendly animal that many people keep as a pet.
mnemonic: ANIMAL — ANIMAted and alive, it moves around.

meaning_en: A response to a question or solution to a problem
example: Can you answer the question on the board?
mnemonic: ANSWER — AN + SWER, when you SWEAR to respond correctly.

meaning_en: To reach a destination after travelling
example: We will arrive at the airport two hours before the flight.
mnemonic: ARRIVE — A RIVER ends somewhere; you arrive at its end.

meaning_en: Creative work such as paintings, drawings, or sculptures
example: She loves art and spends hours painting every weekend.
mnemonic: ART — heART; art comes from the heart.

meaning_en: To say or write something as a question to get information
example: Don't be shy — ask the teacher if you don't understand.
mnemonic: ASK — A Student Knows by asking.

meaning_en: A very young child who cannot yet walk or talk
example: The baby cried loudly when it was hungry.
mnemonic: BABY — BA BA! That's the only sound a baby makes.

meaning_en: Not good; of low quality or harmful
example: Eating too much sugar is bad for your teeth.
mnemonic: BAD — if you feel SAD, things feel BAD too.

meaning_en: Very attractive and pleasing to look at
example: The sunset over the ocean was absolutely beautiful.
mnemonic: BEAUTIFUL — BEAUTY + FUL; full of beauty.

meaning_en: To start to be something different or new
example: She worked hard to become a doctor.
mnemonic: BE + COME — you COME to BE something new.

meaning_en: To start doing something
example: Let's begin the meeting now that everyone is here.
mnemonic: BEGIN — BE + GIN; GIN starts the party — you begin!

meaning_en: To think something is true or real
example: I believe she is telling the truth.
mnemonic: BELIEVE — BE + LIVE in it; you live the truth when you believe.

meaning_en: Large in size, amount, or degree
example: They live in a big house with a large garden.
mnemonic: BIG — B I G letters — each one bigger than the last!

meaning_en: A collection of written pages bound together for reading
example: She read the entire book in one afternoon.
mnemonic: BOOK — BOO + K; a scary story in a BOOK makes you say BOO!

meaning_en: Feeling uninterested and tired due to lack of excitement
example: He was bored during the long lecture.
mnemonic: BORED — a BOARD is flat and dull, just like being bored.

meaning_en: To separate something into pieces by force
example: Be careful not to break the glass.
mnemonic: BREAK — BRick EAKed apart; it BREAKS.

meaning_en: To carry or take something to a place
example: Please bring your passport to the interview.
mnemonic: BRING — a KING says 'BRING me gifts!' — towards him.

meaning_en: To make a structure by putting materials together
example: They want to build a new school in the village.
mnemonic: BUILD — BUILDings are what you build.

meaning_en: To get something by paying money for it
example: I want to buy a new pair of shoes.
mnemonic: BUY — BY giving money, you BUY things.

meaning_en: To contact someone by phone, or to shout out to someone
example: I will call you tonight to discuss the plan.
mnemonic: CALL — C ALL your friends; use a phone to call them all.

meaning_en: To make or become different
example: She decided to change her hairstyle.
mnemonic: CHANGE — CHAINS of habit must break for change to happen.

meaning_en: Low in price; not expensive
example: These shoes are cheap — they only cost 200 rupees.
mnemonic: CHEAP — CHEAT you won't; cheap price is a good deal.

meaning_en: To decide which one you want from a group of options
example: You can choose any book from the shelf.
mnemonic: CHOOSE — CHOO CHOO train picks one track — you choose!

meaning_en: A large and important town with many people and buildings
example: Mumbai is the largest city in India.
mnemonic: CITY — BUSY + CITY; cities are always busy.

meaning_en: Free from dirt or impurities
example: Make sure you clean your room before the guests arrive.
mnemonic: CLEAN — LEAN and spotless; lean means nothing extra, clean means nothing dirty.

meaning_en: Having a low temperature; not warm
example: The water from the tap is very cold in winter.
mnemonic: COLD — COLD = COLDer days in December.

meaning_en: To prepare food using heat
example: She loves to cook traditional Indian food for her family.
mnemonic: COOK — a BOOK in the kitchen tells you how to cook.

meaning_en: Free from mistakes; right
example: All your answers in the test were correct.
mnemonic: CORRECT — CORRECTion comes after a mistake; correct is having no mistakes.

meaning_en: To move your body rhythmically to music
example: She learned to dance Bharatnatyam at the age of five.
mnemonic: DANCE — DAN CEs to every song — he loves to dance.

meaning_en: Likely to cause harm or injury
example: It is dangerous to drive fast in rainy weather.
mnemonic: DANGEROUS — DANGER is the root; full of danger.

meaning_en: To make a choice after thinking about it
example: We need to decide which restaurant to go to.
mnemonic: DECIDE — DE + CIDE — to CUT through options and pick one.

meaning_en: Having a very pleasant taste or smell
example: The biryani at that restaurant is absolutely delicious.
mnemonic: DELICIOUS — DE-LICIOUS sounds like 'Lick-ious'; you want to lick it!

meaning_en: Not the same as another person or thing
example: The two sisters have very different personalities.
mnemonic: DIFFERENT — DIFFER + ENT; things differ from each other.

meaning_en: Not easy; requiring effort or skill
example: The maths exam was very difficult this year.
mnemonic: DIFFICULT — DIFF + I + CULT — it's a different cult to enter; hard to get in.

meaning_en: Not clean; covered in dirt or mess
example: His shoes were dirty after playing football in the mud.
mnemonic: DIRTY — DIRT + Y; full of dirt.

meaning_en: To take liquid into your mouth and swallow it
example: Remember to drink at least eight glasses of water a day.
mnemonic: DRINK — RINK is an ice ring; you drink water to cool down after skating.

meaning_en: To operate a vehicle and make it move
example: She passed her test and can now drive on her own.
mnemonic: DRIVE — DRIVE yourself forward; take the wheel.

meaning_en: Before the expected or usual time
example: She woke up early to catch the morning train.
mnemonic: EARLY — EAR + LY; your EARS wake you EARLY with an alarm.

meaning_en: Not difficult; done without much effort
example: The first chapter of the book was easy to understand.
mnemonic: EASY — EASE + Y; full of ease.

meaning_en: To put food in your mouth and swallow it
example: You should eat breakfast before going to school.
mnemonic: EAT — 'E' + 'AT'; you ARE at the table to EAT.

meaning_en: To get pleasure from something
example: I really enjoy watching films on rainy days.
mnemonic: ENJOY — EN + JOY; fill yourself with joy.

meaning_en: Feeling very happy and enthusiastic about something
example: The children were excited about the school trip.
mnemonic: EXCITED — EXIT + CITED; you can't wait to exit and shout about it!

meaning_en: Causing excitement; thrilling
example: The match was so exciting that no one could sit still.
mnemonic: EXCITING — something that EXCITEs you is EXCITING.

meaning_en: Costing a lot of money
example: That phone is too expensive for me to buy right now.
mnemonic: EXPENSIVE — EXPENSE + IVE; full of expense.

meaning_en: To drop down from a higher position
example: Be careful not to fall off the ladder.
mnemonic: FALL — leaves FALL in autumn; gravity pulls everything down.

meaning_en: Known by many people around the world
example: Sachin Tendulkar is a famous cricketer.
mnemonic: FAMOUS — FAME + OUS; having fame makes you famous.

meaning_en: Moving or happening quickly
example: The cheetah is the fastest land animal in the world.
mnemonic: FAST — a FAST car ZOOMS past; speed = fast.

meaning_en: To experience an emotion or physical sensation
example: I feel happy when I spend time with my friends.
mnemonic: FEEL — you use your HEEL to FEEL the ground below.

meaning_en: To discover something after looking for it
example: I can't find my keys — have you seen them?
mnemonic: FIND — FINE + D; you find something and feel fine about it.

meaning_en: To complete something or come to the end of it
example: Did you finish all your homework before dinner?
mnemonic: FINISH — the FINISH LINE is the end of a race.

meaning_en: To move through the air using wings or an engine
example: Birds fly south in winter to escape the cold.
mnemonic: FLY — a FLY insect flies; small wings, big journey.

meaning_en: To go after someone or move in the same direction
example: Follow the arrows to reach the exit.
mnemonic: FOLLOW — HOLLOW path; you follow the path that others made hollow.

meaning_en: To fail to remember something
example: Don't forget to bring your lunch tomorrow.
mnemonic: FORGET — FOR + GET; you forget to GET what you needed.

meaning_en: Not costing anything; or not restricted
example: Entry to the museum is free on Sundays.
mnemonic: FREE — FREEdom means no chains; free costs nothing.

meaning_en: Kind and pleasant towards other people
example: The hotel staff were very friendly and helpful.
mnemonic: FRIENDLY — FRIEND + LY; behaving like a friend.

meaning_en: Causing laughter or amusement
example: He told a funny joke that made everyone laugh.
mnemonic: FUNNY — FUN + NY; full of fun.

meaning_en: To hand something to someone else
example: Please give me your homework by Friday.
mnemonic: GIVE — G + IVE; give life to others by giving things.

meaning_en: To move from one place to another
example: Let's go to the park this afternoon.
mnemonic: GO — a traffic light turns GREEN; GO!

meaning_en: Of a high quality or standard; pleasant
example: She is a very good student who always studies hard.
mnemonic: GOOD — GOD is GOOD; all things from God are good.

meaning_en: Very good; much more than average
example: That was a great film — I loved every minute of it.
mnemonic: GREAT — GREATer than the rest; exceeds everyone.

meaning_en: Feeling pleasure or joy
example: She was happy when she heard she got the job.
mnemonic: HAPPY — HAP + PY; when good things HAP-pen, you feel happy.

meaning_en: Difficult to do; or solid and firm
example: The exam was hard, but she managed to pass.
mnemonic: HARD — a HARD rock is difficult to break; hard = tough.

meaning_en: To feel a very strong dislike for something or someone
example: I hate waking up early in the morning.
mnemonic: HATE — opposite of 'great'; if you hate it, it's not great.

meaning_en: To make it easier for someone to do something
example: Can you help me carry these heavy bags?
mnemonic: HELP — YELP for help; when in trouble, you yelp for help.

meaning_en: Far above the ground or average level
example: The kite flew high above the rooftops.
mnemonic: HIGH — H + IGH; IGH sounds like sky — high in the sky.

meaning_en: To wish for something to happen
example: I hope the weather is nice for our picnic tomorrow.
mnemonic: HOPE — ROPE of hope; you hold onto hope like a rope.

meaning_en: Having a high temperature
example: Be careful — the tea is still very hot.
mnemonic: HOT — a H-OT pot on a stove is hot.

meaning_en: Feeling the need to eat food
example: I am so hungry — I haven't eaten anything since morning.
mnemonic: HUNGRY — HUNGER + Y; full of hunger.

meaning_en: Having great significance or value
example: It is important to drink enough water every day.
mnemonic: IMPORTANT — IMPORT + ANT; importing something big is important.

meaning_en: Wanting to know or learn about something
example: She is interested in learning how to paint.
mnemonic: INTERESTED — INTEREST + ED; you showed interest.

meaning_en: Holding your attention; worth knowing
example: That documentary about space was really interesting.
mnemonic: INTERESTING — INTERESTing; it holds your interest.

meaning_en: To continue having or holding something
example: Keep this letter safe — you may need it later.
mnemonic: KEEP — DEEP inside your pocket; you keep it safe.

meaning_en: To have information about something in your mind
example: Do you know the answer to this question?
mnemonic: KNOW — KNOWledge is what you KNOW.

meaning_en: Big in size or amount
example: They moved into a large house with five bedrooms.
mnemonic: LARGE — an enLARGEd photo is large.

meaning_en: After the expected or correct time
example: Sorry I'm late — I missed the bus.
mnemonic: LATE — a PLATE arrives LATE at the table.

meaning_en: To make sounds that show you think something is funny
example: We all laughed at his funny impression of the teacher.
mnemonic: LAUGH — LAUGH sounds like 'laff'; you laff when it's funny.

meaning_en: To gain knowledge or skill through study or experience
example: It takes time to learn a new language.
mnemonic: LEARN — EARN knowledge; you EARN skills when you LEARN.

meaning_en: To go away from a place or person
example: What time do you leave for school in the morning?
mnemonic: LEAVE — LEAF falls off a tree — it LEAVEs the branch.

meaning_en: To find something pleasant or enjoyable
example: I like listening to music while studying.
mnemonic: LIKE — a BIKE ride; you like riding a bike.

meaning_en: To pay attention to sound
example: Please listen carefully to the instructions.
mnemonic: LISTEN — rearranges to SILENT; to listen, you must be silent.

meaning_en: To have your home in a particular place; or to be alive
example: She lives near the railway station.
mnemonic: LIVE — spell it backwards: EVIL; to live is the opposite of evil (death).

meaning_en: Measuring a great distance from one end to another
example: It was a long drive through the mountains.
mnemonic: LONG — a song that goes on too LONG.

meaning_en: To direct your eyes towards something
example: Look at that beautiful rainbow in the sky!
mnemonic: LOOK — two O's are the eyes that LOOK.

meaning_en: To no longer have something, or to fail to win
example: She was upset when her team lost the match.
mnemonic: LOSE — LOSEr doesn't keep what they had.

meaning_en: A deep feeling of affection for someone
example: She loves her family more than anything else.
mnemonic: LOVE — DOVE; the dove is a symbol of love.

meaning_en: To create or produce something
example: Can you make a cup of tea for me please?
mnemonic: MAKE — a CAKE you MAKE; create something delicious.

meaning_en: To come together with someone for the first time or by arrangement
example: Let's meet at the coffee shop at noon.
mnemonic: MEET — SWEET to MEET someone new.

meaning_en: Relating to the present time; up to date
example: The city has many modern buildings with glass walls.
mnemonic: MODERN — MODE + RN; the latest mode or fashion is modern.

meaning_en: To change position or go to a new place
example: The family decided to move to a new city for work.
mnemonic: MOVE — GROOVE means to move rhythmically.

meaning_en: To require something because it is essential
example: You need a visa to travel to some countries.
mnemonic: NEED — you FEED a NEED; if you're hungry, you need food.

meaning_en: Recently made, bought, or discovered
example: She got a new phone as a birthday gift.
mnemonic: NEW — NEWS is always NEW information.

meaning_en: Pleasant or enjoyable; kind towards others
example: What a nice surprise to see you here!
mnemonic: NICE — ICE CREAM is NICE; sweet and pleasant.

meaning_en: Having existed for a long time; not new or young
example: The old man had lived in the village his whole life.
mnemonic: OLD — GOLD gets more valuable when OLD.

meaning_en: Not closed or sealed; to make something accessible
example: Can you open the window? It's too hot in here.
mnemonic: OPEN — O + PEN; an OPEN pen has no cap.

meaning_en: To give money in exchange for goods or services
example: Don't forget to pay the electricity bill this month.
mnemonic: PAY — PAY DAY is the day you get and pay money.

meaning_en: To take part in a game or activity for fun
example: The children play cricket in the park every evening.
mnemonic: PLAY — CLAY — children use clay to PLAY.

meaning_en: Liked or enjoyed by many people
example: Cricket is a very popular sport in India.
mnemonic: POPULAR — POPUL + AR; the PEOPLE (population) love it.

meaning_en: To place something in a particular position
example: Please put your phone away during the exam.
mnemonic: PUT — PUT it down; stop and place it.

meaning_en: Done with speed; fast
example: We had a quick lunch and then went back to work.
mnemonic: QUICK — TICK TOCK; a clock goes QUICK when time flies.

meaning_en: Making little or no noise; calm and peaceful
example: Please be quiet — the baby is sleeping.
mnemonic: QUIET — QUILT is soft and silent; like a quiet room.

meaning_en: To look at written words and understand them
example: She reads a book before going to sleep every night.
mnemonic: READ — RED pen; teachers READ and mark in RED.

meaning_en: Prepared and able to do something
example: Are you ready to leave? The taxi is waiting.
mnemonic: READY — STEADY, READY, GO! Ready comes before going.

meaning_en: Actually existing, not imagined or fake
example: Is that a real diamond, or is it glass?
mnemonic: REAL — REALLY; if it's really there, it's real.

meaning_en: To bring something back to your mind
example: Do you remember where you put your glasses?
mnemonic: REMEMBER — RE + MEMBER; a team MEMBER you always RE-call.

meaning_en: Having a lot of money or valuable possessions
example: He became rich after starting his own company.
mnemonic: RICH — RICH food is also expensive, like a rich person.

meaning_en: To move quickly on foot
example: She runs five kilometres every morning.
mnemonic: RUN — RUN for the BUS before it leaves!

meaning_en: Feeling unhappy or sorrowful
example: She was sad when her best friend moved away.
mnemonic: SAD — BAD things make you feel SAD.

meaning_en: To speak words aloud to express something
example: What did she say when she heard the news?
mnemonic: SAY — a JAY bird SAYs things loudly.

meaning_en: To notice or observe something using your eyes
example: Can you see the mountains from your window?
mnemonic: SEE — 3 E's; your two EYES SEE everything.

meaning_en: To cause something to go to a place or person
example: Please send me the document by email.
mnemonic: SEND — FRIEND sends a SEND; friends send messages.

meaning_en: Small in length or height; lasting a brief time
example: The talk was short but very informative.
mnemonic: SHORT — a SHORT shirt doesn't reach your waist.

meaning_en: To let someone see something; to demonstrate
example: Can you show me how to use this app?
mnemonic: SHOW — a FLOW SHOW; water flows to show its power.

meaning_en: Physically ill or unwell
example: She stayed home because she was sick with a cold.
mnemonic: SICK — when you LICK dirty things, you get SICK.

meaning_en: To make music with your voice
example: She loves to sing in the shower every morning.
mnemonic: SING — a RING; when a bell rings, it sings.

meaning_en: To rest in an unconscious state

mnemonic: SLEEP — SHEEP; counting SHEEP helps you SLEEP.

meaning_en: Not moving or happening quickly
example: The internet connection is very slow today.
mnemonic: SLOW — a SNAIL is SLOW; S-L-O-W goes the snail.

meaning_en: Little in size or amount
example: She lives in a small flat near the city centre.
mnemonic: SMALL — a BALL can be SMALL; think of a tiny bouncy ball.

meaning_en: To say words aloud; to communicate using your voice
example: Can you speak more slowly, please?
mnemonic: SPEAK — a BEAK on a bird; birds speak with their beak.

meaning_en: To begin doing something
example: The match will start at 6 PM.
mnemonic: START — STAR + T; every STAR starts shining from a small point.

meaning_en: To no longer continue an action
example: Stop talking and listen to the teacher.
mnemonic: STOP — a red traffic light means STOP.

meaning_en: Having great physical power or ability
example: You need to be strong to lift heavy weights.
mnemonic: STRONG — a PRONG is a STRONG fork shape; strong structure.

meaning_en: To spend time learning about a subject
example: I need to study hard for my exams next week.
mnemonic: STUDY — BUDDY; find a study BUDDY to learn faster.

meaning_en: Having a greater than average height
example: Basketball players are usually very tall.
mnemonic: TALL — a WALL is tall; it stands high above you.

meaning_en: To help someone learn something
example: She teaches mathematics to students in Class 8.
mnemonic: TEACH — REACH; a teacher can REACH and TEACH everyone.

meaning_en: To communicate information to someone
example: Please tell me when the meeting is.
mnemonic: TELL — BELL; ring a BELL to TELL everyone something.

meaning_en: To use your mind to consider ideas
example: Take time to think before you answer.
mnemonic: THINK — the THINKER statue — a man sits and THINKS.

meaning_en: Feeling you need rest or sleep
example: She was tired after working all day.
mnemonic: TIRED — HIRED all day; working non-stop leaves you tired.

meaning_en: To go from one place to another, often over a distance
example: She loves to travel and has visited 20 countries.
mnemonic: TRAVEL — GRAVEL road; you travel on a gravel road.

meaning_en: In accordance with fact; not false

mnemonic: TRUE — BLUE; true blue is a phrase meaning completely true.

meaning_en: To attempt to do something

mnemonic: TRY — a FRY tries a new recipe; you try new things.

meaning_en: To know the meaning of something

mnemonic: UNDERSTAND — UNDER + STAND; to stand under someone's ideas means to understand them.

meaning_en: To do something with an object for a purpose

meaning_en: To go to a place or person for a period of time
example: We plan to visit the Taj Mahal this summer.
mnemonic: VISIT — VISITor comes to VISIT; they are a visitor.

meaning_en: To stay in one place until something happens

mnemonic: WAIT — GATE; wait at the GATE for your turn.

meaning_en: To move on foot at a regular pace

mnemonic: WALK — CHALK; draw a path with chalk and WALK on it.

meaning_en: To have a desire for something
example: I want to become an engineer when I grow up.
mnemonic: WANT — RANT; you RANT about what you WANT.

meaning_en: Somewhat hot; comfortable in temperature

mnemonic: WARM — a FARM in the sun is WARM.

meaning_en: To look at something carefully over time
example: I like to watch documentaries about nature.

meaning_en: To be the best or first in a competition
example: Our team worked very hard to win the championship.
mnemonic: WIN — SPIN the wheel of fortune; spin to WIN.

meaning_en: To do a job or task; to function properly

mnemonic: WORK — a FORK works hard to lift food.

meaning_en: To form letters or words on a surface
